title: Sour Fluffy Brazilian Vermicelli StirFry
description: A bright, tangy stirfry featuring airy vermicelli, fresh veggies, and a splash of lime for a quick solo dinner.
instructions: Cook vermicelli in boiling salted water until al dente, about 3–4 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water; set aside.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat and add the vegetable oil. Once shimmering, add minced garlic and grated ginger, stir‑fry 30 seconds.
Add sliced carrots and bell pepper; stir‑fry for 1–2 minutes until slightly softened but still crisp.
Push vegetables to the side, pour beaten egg into the center, scramble until just set, then mix with the vegetables.
Toss in cooked vermicelli, drizzle soy sauce and lime juice, stir‑fry for another 1–2 minutes until everything is heated through and evenly coated.
If desired, add a pinch of sugar to balance the sourness; stir well.
Remove from heat, stir in chopped green onions and cilantro.
Plate immediately; the dish will be bright, sour, and fluffy from the egg and light stir‑fry method.
Serve hot as a solo dinner.
